framework_alignment: FEMA Continuity Guidance Circular (Alaska non-federal entity guidance)

Risk Services is charged with designing and implementing emergency preparedness, disaster and business continuity programs system-wide (UAA/UAF/UAS). Page resolves on official alaska.edu host; quote taken from the official program page (web_fetch rate-limited, so link-only).

plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
evidence_quote: we prioritize the assurance of academic continuity in the event of an emergency disruption that may result in the temporary or long-term suspension of campus activities, including face-to-face classes.
catalog_notes: Alabama A&M 'Academic Continuity' program (Distance Education & Extended Studies) with Students, Faculty, Staff and FAQ resources and a linked IT Continuity page. Frames academic continuity as protecting the institution's mission and resiliency during emergency disruptions.

framework_alignment: Continuity of Operations (COOP) essential-functions model
evidence_quote: The objectives of a Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) are to minimize loss to the University, continue to serve faculty, staff, students, and visitors, and maintain administrative operations. The process encompasses maintaining and recovering the business, not just the recovery of technology.
catalog_notes: Program page managed by the University Police Department / Emergency Preparedness. COOP is executed after life-safety issues are addressed by the EOP. Departments complete a fillable COOP Template PDF (linked on the page) forwarded to Emergency Management. Note: jsu.edu is Jacksonville State University (Alabama), distinct from Jackson State University (jsums.edu, Mississippi).

catalog_notes: Tuskegee University (private HBCU). IT Disaster Recovery Plan PDF resolves on the official tuskegee.edu host (Information Technology). institution_name: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
framework_alignment: Business risk management / operational risk management (tiered IT recovery)
evidence_quote: The Business Continuity Plan (BCP) for the University of Arkansas at Little Rock (UALR) integrates business risk management, operational risk management, and business continuity.
catalog_notes: UA Little Rock Information Technology Services publishes a Business Continuity Plan (Third Revision, 2010) using a tiered approach to keep the university managed during a disaster that renders the technical infrastructure inoperable for a period exceeding two days. Distinct institution from University of Arkansas at Monticello already cataloged. Page resolves on official ualr.edu host; quote taken from the official ITS BCP page (web_fetch rate-limited, so link-only).

framework_alignment: All-hazards Emergency Management Plan; administrative function continuity
evidence_quote: University of Arkansas at Monticello (UAM) - An Emergency Management Plan that includes maintaining a Business Continuity Plan (BCP) identifying systems needed to conduct administrative functions such as procurement, accounts payable, payroll, and student registration.

Quote is from the search-result summary describing the embedded Business Continuity Plan covering administrative functions (procurement, AP, payroll, registration). Direct PDF render not completed at access time due to fetch rate limiting; verify the verbatim in-document phrasing on next render.

evidence_quote: ASU's COOP ensures critical university functions continue during disruptions or unexpected events. Each unit assigns a Continuity Coordinator to maintain its plan with Emergency Management.
catalog_notes: Arizona State University Continuity of Operations Program managed through the CFO/University Business Services Office of Risk and Emergency Management. Tier-based model: Tier I units (vital to core operations) must maintain a standalone plan; Tier II units (can pause up to 72 hours) are managed by Tier I divisions. Each unit assigns a Continuity Coordinator; plans stored in an internal COOP SharePoint, reviewed annually and updated biennially.

evidence_quote: This document is intended to provide guidance and assistance for all NAU Colleges and Departments in the development, implementation and maintenance of a business continuity program.
catalog_notes: NAU Comptroller's Office maintains the university Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plan. Detailed Business Impact Analysis methodology with Maximum Acceptable Outage (MAO) recovery targets and classification of core processes into Critical, Essential, and Administrative functions. Publishes a BCDR glossary, disaster recovery steps, a critical-functions assessment template, and links to the Arizona DOA business continuity standard.

evidence_quote: To be better prepared, all UA departments, units, and colleges are required to use this form to complete a Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) to describe how your area would operate during a wide range of emergencies or influenza pandemic and recover afterward to be fully operational.
catalog_notes: University of Arizona Emergency Management (Critical Incident Response Team) COOP program. Departments/units/colleges complete a COOP via a Qualtrics-based form and can download a PDF; a sample COOP is published via Box. Operating units develop Business Continuity Plans identifying impacts of losses, recovery strategies, and Emergency Operations Personnel.

institution_name: California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo
framework_alignment: Federal Continuity Directives FCD 1 and FCD 2; Campus Administrative Policy 383; California EO S-04-06; California Emergency Services Act
evidence_quote: We follow guidance from the Federal Continuity Directives (FCD 1 and FCD 2) issued by FEMA. Our programmatic policy is outlined in Campus Administrative Policy 383. The California State University System requires every CSU campus to maintain an active continuity program.
catalog_notes: Department of Emergency Management Business Continuity Program with a detailed table of 16 Mission Essential Functions mapped to divisions/units. BCP explicitly plans for devolution (adapting if resources are lost) and reconstitution (rebuilding when stabilized). Uses Kuali Ready; oversight by the Business Continuity Planning Committee (BCPC); approved BCPs stored in SharePoint repository.

framework_alignment: CSU Executive Order 1014 Business Continuity Policy; Federal Preparedness Circular 65; EO 1069 Risk Management
evidence_quote: The purpose of CSU Executive Order 1014 is to maintain an ongoing program on each campus that ensures the continuity of essential functions or operations following a catastrophic event or disruption of services. A business continuity plan (BCP) is a process of developing and documenting arrangements and procedures that enable an organization to respond to an event and return to performing its essential functions as quickly as possible.
catalog_notes: Cal Maritime's own Department Business Continuity Plan guide/template, published by Safety & Risk Management for campus departments to complete. References EO 1014 and Federal Preparedness Circular 65; Cal Maritime uses the term Critical Function for Essential Functions. Plan tested in part annually and fully every seven years. Hosted on the official maritime-archive.calpoly.edu domain following the Cal Maritime/Cal Poly integration.

framework_alignment: CSU Executive Order 1014; coordinates with campus Emergency Operations Plan (EOP)
evidence_quote: This Business Continuity Plan (BCP) was designed to facilitate the continuing operation of the University following a natural or man-made disaster, or other business-interrupting event. The BCP defines the groups who will be responsible for the post-disaster evaluation of the status of critical University systems and the planning and design for post-disaster University operations.
catalog_notes: 50-page full BCP with appendices for Order of Succession (Appendix A), Delegations of Authority (section 3.0), Essential Activities & Functions (Appendix D), alternate facility (4.5.6), testing intervals, and after-action reports. Classes incidents into single-building, catastrophic, and communicable-disease scenarios.

framework_alignment: CSU Business Continuity Program Executive Order 1014
evidence_quote: Per the CSU Business Continuity Program Executive Order 1014 the elements of a Business Continuity Program are: 1. Identifying Departments That Perform Essential Services 2. Establishing and Maintaining a Framework for Business Continuity 3. Training 4. Completing a Business Impact Analysis and Risk Assessment 5. Creation of Departmental Business Continuity Plans 6. Business Continuity Plan Review 7. Business Continuity Plan Testing
catalog_notes: Program overview document presenting the seven EO 1014 elements; uses the Chico Ready (Kuali Ready) software framework. EO 1014 requires a portion of each plan tested every year and the entire plan tested once every 7 years.

framework_alignment: California EO S-04-06; CSU System Continuity Program Policy (EO 1014)
evidence_quote: The Business Continuity Plan (BCP) establishes the basis by which CSUEB will prepare, organize, and respond to a disruption in operation of essential functions. CSUEB's Department BCPs may include several elements, such as the below... Business Impact Analysis (BIA) Summary, which include a list of the Department's main essential function(s)... Order of Succession and Delegations of Authority, identifying a list of primary University personnel, including successors, to ensure there is no lapse in the decision making authority
catalog_notes: Risk Management business continuity program covering the Hayward and Concord campuses. Department BCP elements explicitly include BIA summary, personnel accountability, and orders of succession and delegations of authority. References EO S-04-06 and EO 1014.

framework_alignment: CSU Policy/Executive Order 1014 (CSU System Continuity Program)
evidence_quote: This program encompasses academic, business, and information technology (IT) continuity of operations plans (COOPs) which support the safety of students, staff, and faculty while also enabling the earliest possible recovery and return to regular university operations after an emergency.
catalog_notes: CSU Fullerton Continuity Program dated January 1, 2026 (82 pages). Comprehensive program with Delegation of Authority, Risk Assessment, Business Impact Analysis with RTO, Essential Functions & Critical Systems, business-unit COOPs, a Multi-Year Training & Exercise Plan, a Crisis Communication Plan, a Business Unit Continuity Plan Template (Appendix D), and CSU Policy 1014 (Annex C). Covers academic, business and IT continuity.

plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
evidence_quote: this guide was created in case circumstances might require a class to temporarily be offered in an alternative mode with minimal notice. This pertains to a campus closure, increased absenteeism during a flu outbreak, or a family emergency.
catalog_notes: CSULB 'Academic Continuity' Resource Center providing faculty guidance and tools (including a 'Loss of Instructional Time' resource) to shift a class to an alternative instructional mode on short notice during a campus closure or other disruption.

framework_alignment: CSU System Continuity Program Policy
evidence_quote: Continuity is more than just the ability to recover from a disaster; it's the ability to continue operating before, during, and after an incident. That's why it's critical to understand, anticipate, and plan for events that threaten our mission so that Cal State Monterey Bay remains viable.
catalog_notes: Continuity planning program using the OtterReady (Kuali Ready) system. Links to an Enterprise Continuity of Operations Plan (E-COOP), CSU System Continuity Program Policy, Continuity Advisory Group Charge, Critical Functions Summary Guide, and After Action Report Survey. Continuity covers instruction, research, and essential services. CSUMB is a federally designated Hispanic-Serving Institution.

framework_alignment: CSU Chancellor's Office Executive Order #1014
evidence_quote: The CSU Chancellor's Office, in Executive Order #1014, delegates each campus the responsibility for implementing and maintaining an ongoing business continuity program to ensure the continuity of essential functions or operations following or during the recovery phase of a catastrophic event.
catalog_notes: Risk Management Services business continuity program using the Sac State Ready web-based Business Continuity Planner database for all campus departments to enter, store, and update continuity plans. Page also documents a separate Emergency Action Program (8 CCR 3220).

framework_alignment: CSU System Continuity Program Executive Order 1014
evidence_quote: The Campus Continuity Program provides a structured framework to help Stan State sustain and restore essential functions during and after adverse events. This is achieved through continuity of operations and departmental business continuity plans, which outline strategies to mitigate disruptions, maintain critical services, and recover swiftly.
catalog_notes: Campus Continuity Program page linking to a public Continuity of Operations (COOP) PDF, StanReady (Kuali Ready) guide, Academic Continuity Planning Guide, Guide to Critical Functions, and Annual Review Checklist hosted on official Stan State SharePoint. Each business unit must align its plan with the University's essential functions.

system_affiliation: San Mateo County Community College District / California Community Colleges
plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
evidence_quote: This plan ensures that instructional activities continue uninterrupted during emergencies that disrupt normal college operations. Cañada College has developed strategies to maintain academic integrity, support faculty and students, and ensure equitable access to learning.
catalog_notes: 8-page Emergency Instructional Continuity Plan posted by the Office of Instruction. Covers transition to Canvas/Zoom remote learning, equitable technology access, alternative assessments, technology-failure backups, clear communication channels, and a recovery/resumption of on-campus instruction phase. Document is marked DRAFT (1.16.2025).

evidence_quote: The primary objective of the CLPCCD Disaster Recovery plan is to protect and safeguard the District’s Information Technology resources, including the network infrastructure, servers, applications, and data, and to ensure the ability to function effectively and ensure business continuity in the event of a disruption to normal operating procedures.
catalog_notes: 34-page districtwide ITS Disaster Recovery Plan covering Chabot College (Hayward), Las Positas College (Livermore), the EDCE site (Pleasanton), and the District Office (Dublin). Documents response, recovery, resumption, restoration, return, and a defined reconstitution mechanism. Marked 'Limited Distribution Only' but publicly posted on the district site.

system_affiliation: Coast Community College District / California Community Colleges
evidence_quote: The IT Disaster Recover (DR) plan for the Orange Coast College (OCC), a member of the Coast Community College District (CCCD), sets the direction and priorities on how the CCCD IT organization will proactively protect the OCC IT assets prior to a disaster; how best to operate during and after a disaster and how best to protect the mission critical services that will provide OCC with the best chance of survival.
catalog_notes: IT Disaster Recovery Plan plus IT Security Standards and Protocols, published as Exhibit 120 in OCC's 2016 Accreditation Midterm Report. Covers enterprise backup/offsite tape vaulting, dual data centers with CRAC/UPS/generator redundancy, network/telecom recovery, and annual DR testing by the Infrastructure Emergency Response Team (IERT).

framework_alignment: CSU Executive Order 1014; Federal Preparedness Circular 65
evidence_quote: A business continuity plan (BCP) is a process of developing and documenting arrangements and procedures that enable an organization to respond to an event and return to performing its essential functions as quickly as possible
catalog_notes: SDSU departmental Business Continuity Plan template/document required under CSU Executive Order 1014. Defines essential functions per Federal Preparedness Circular 65, includes Essential Functions Worksheets with maximum downtime (RTO), Key Personnel and Vendor contact appendices, plan maintenance/review log, and BCP Coordinator approval. Note: cites FPC-65 (an essential-functions framework), not FCD-1.

framework_alignment: CSU Chancellor's Office Executive Order #1014; three-phase inception cycle
evidence_quote: SJSU Business Continuity Planning (BCP) is the creation of a logistical plan for how the University will partially or completely restore certain interrupted essential/critical departments (functions) within a predetermined time after a disaster or disruption.
catalog_notes: Detailed BCP Master Plan covering Business Impact Analysis, risk/vulnerabilities assessment, recovery requirements, six-step chain-of-command/succession (Presidential Directive 2009-03), delegation of authority to VP Admin/Finance, vital records, recovery time objectives, alternate work sites, testing tiers (walk-through to live simulation), records retention (4 years), and training. Entire plan tested once every 7 years.

framework_alignment: UC Davis PPM 390-10 Campus Emergency Policy; UC Ready systemwide program
evidence_quote: Per PPM 390-10, Campus Emergency Policy, each unit and department at UC Davis must sustain "a program to create, maintain, update, and annually test Continuity of Operations (COOP) (i.e., mission continuity) plans."
catalog_notes: Safety Services Emergency Preparedness planning program. COOP/mission-continuity plans are mandated for each unit by PPM 390-10 and address communication, relocation, changing operational status, and information system outages, using the UC Ready tool. Quote taken from the official UC Davis Safety Services search-result snippet; full page bytes not rendered in this session due to fetch rate limits.

framework_alignment: UC Ready systemwide continuity program; business impact analysis
evidence_quote: A business continuity plan is used once life and safety are secure and is used to help you to continue your operations. These plans also work with IT disaster recovery plans which are plans and procedures to restore IT services (e.g. applications and infrastructure) as part of the overall campus business continuity plan.
catalog_notes: Insurance & Risk Management business continuity program using UC Ready (Fusion) tool across 10 UC campuses and 5 medical centers. Three-step process: business impact analysis (priorities), strategies, and plans. IT Disaster Recovery templates available. Provides a downloadable sample Business Continuity Plan.

framework_alignment: UC Ready systemwide continuity program
evidence_quote: Key essential functions that continuity planning prepares the University for in the event of an incident are communication, relocation, system outages, and change in operational status.
catalog_notes: Risk Services business continuity program using the UC Ready tool. Quote taken from the official UC Merced Risk Services search-result snippet; full page bytes not rendered in this session due to fetch rate limits.

framework_alignment: UC Ready systemwide continuity program
evidence_quote: Business continuity planning helps an organization continue to provide essential functions or quickly resume essential functions in the event of a disruption. Examples of disruptions may range from earthquakes, wildfires, power outages, hazardous material spills, to gas and water leaks. Continuity planning prepares UC San Diego to continue its mission of teaching, research, public service, and patient care despite adverse events.
catalog_notes: Emergency Management & Mission Continuity program using the systemwide UC Ready tool; offers a downloadable essential-function-based Business Continuity Plan template (Excel). Plans identify departmental essential functions and resources (IT, facility, personnel, equipment).

framework_alignment: UCSF Mission Continuity Program (UC Ready)
evidence_quote: The mission continuity planning process begins with determining core essential functions and the departments that comprise them. These are functions that must be recovered within days or weeks of a disaster or major disruption in order to prevent irreparable harm to the University's ability to provide critical patient care, administrative, academic, research or service functions or the University's reputation.
catalog_notes: Enterprise Emergency Management (Be Ready) Mission Continuity Program. Continuity measures include resuming functions at alternate locations, offsite data backup, and maintaining critical contact information. Health-sciences focused (patient care, research). Departmental essential-staffing plans.

framework_alignment: All-Hazards approach; NIMS/ICS; institutional Emergency Management policy
evidence_quote: All schools and departments are required to develop and maintain Business Continuity Plans ("BCP") that describes the unit's critical operations and how each function will continue or quickly resume after a disaster event. The university's priority is to restore teaching, research, patient care, and other mission-critical activities in a timely manner. Additionally, all schools and departments that operate IT systems in support of university business operations, must develop and implement a Disaster Recovery Plan ("DRP") in conjunction with the BCP.
catalog_notes: USC institutional Emergency Management policy mandating department/school Business Continuity Plans and IT Disaster Recovery Plans, overseen by a Business Continuity Steering Committee (chaired by the Senior VP, Administration) and a Crisis Management Team, with FSEP monitoring compliance and facilitating regular testing. The detailed Emergency Response Operations Plan and plan templates are available upon request (not published).

framework_alignment: NIMS/ICS; all-hazards (paired with EOP)
evidence_quote: Business Continuity Planning (BCP) allows departments to continue or rapidly resume mission critical functions like teaching, research, or student housing.
catalog_notes: Risk Management & Insurance Business Continuity Planning page. Could not render page bytes during this session due to a fetch rate limit; URL resolves on the official colostate.edu host and the quoted text is reproduced in the search result snippet for the page. Distinguishes BCP (resume mission-critical functions) from the Emergency Operations Plan (protect life/secure facilities).

framework_alignment: FEMA Continuity of Operations (all-hazards)
evidence_quote: A COOP Plan establishes a framework for processes and procedures to ensure critical mission essential functions continue during a disaster, and resources and personnel are in place, fostering recovering and resiliency.
catalog_notes: University Risk Management COOP program for CU system administration. Requires each department to develop and implement a BCP; defines COOP, Business Impact Analysis/Risk Assessment, Critical Business Functions, and Disaster Recovery Plan. Uses BoldPlanning.com (ContinuityCU.com) software; links formal COOP policy and BIA guidelines PDFs.

framework_alignment: FEMA Federal Continuity Directive elements
evidence_quote: A Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) will document how the division or department will perform essential operations during an emergency situation or long-term disruption, which might last from 2 days to several weeks. The plan will identify mission-critical functions, departmental communication methods, and alternate personnel, systems and locations.
catalog_notes: Department of Emergency and Safety Services COOP overview/guidance document hosted on the official UCCS domain. Explicitly enumerates the FCD continuity elements: Essential Functions, Orders of Succession, Delegations of Authority, Continuity (Alternate) Facilities, Continuity Communications, Vital Records Management, and Human Capital.

institution_name: University of Colorado Denver | Anschutz Medical Campus
framework_alignment: FEMA emergency-management phases (preparedness, mitigation, response, recovery, continuity)
evidence_quote: The division provides planning & preparedness for all schools & colleges during all phases of emergencies (preparedness, mitigation, response, recovery, and continuity of operations or COOP).
catalog_notes: CU Denver/Anschutz Emergency Management Division COOP resource page. Could not render page bytes during this session due to a fetch rate limit; URL resolves on the official ucdenver.edu host and the quoted text is reproduced in the search result snippet. A Comprehensive Emergency Management Plan (CEMP) PDF is also published on the same domain.

framework_alignment: UConn Information Security policy; RTO/RPO
evidence_quote: Each University department will maintain a current, written and tested Business Continuity Plan (BCP) that addresses the department's response to unexpected events that disrupt normal business (for example, fire, vandalism, system failure, and natural disaster). The BCP will include a Disaster Recovery (DR) Plan that addresses maintaining business processes and services in the event of a disaster and the eventual restoration of normal operations.
catalog_notes: UConn 'Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery, Information Technology' policy (Information Security Office). Requires each department to maintain a tested BCP plus DR plan addressing critical systems and data, recovery point/time objectives (RPO/RTO), emergency data access, and annual testing (desk audits, tabletop, walkthroughs, live simulations). Applies to all campuses except UConn Health.

framework_alignment: NIMS/ICS; all-hazards emergency response
evidence_quote: This Emergency Response Plan (ERP) is an “all hazards” plan that addresses emergencies including, but not limited to fire, explosions, chemical spills, oil spills, natural disasters such as floods, hurricanes, tornados, blizzards, and earthquakes, pandemic, and other emergencies such as power outages, mass casualty and active shooter incidents.
catalog_notes: Full Wesleyan University Emergency Response and Business Continuity Plan (Revision 8, May 2014) prepared by the Business Continuity Planning Committee. All-hazards, NIMS/ICS-structured with phase-based sustained actions (Increased Readiness, Emergency, Recovery), ICS position checklists and forms, training/exercises, and embedded emergency operating guidelines (BCP-01 through 07). ~3,300 students, ~1,000 employees. Submitted to CT Dept of Homeland Security.

framework_alignment: Essential-functions continuity; Veoci platform
evidence_quote: A Business Continuity Plan (BCP) is a collection of resources, actions, procedures, and information that is developed, tested, and held in readiness for use in the event of a disaster or major disruption of operations.
catalog_notes: Yale Office of Emergency Management business continuity planning program. Four-step process (determine essential functions, identify required resources, develop recovery strategies/tasks). Departments document plans in Veoci. Dedicated tracks for general audiences, laboratories/research facilities, and clinical practices. Published BCP Quick Start Guide and General Audience guide PDFs.

framework_alignment: All-hazards approach (emergency management plan); FEMA-aligned
evidence_quote: Howard University must ensure its operations are performed efficiently with minimal disruption through a wide range of emergencies. The Continuity of Operations Planning Program ensures that the organization is capable of conducting its essential mission and functions under all threats and conditions.
catalog_notes: Leading private HBCU. Department of Public Safety Office of Emergency Management runs the COOP program and publishes the all-hazards Howard University Emergency Management Plan (HU EMP) as a PDF on the official host. Plans page describes COOP alongside the EMP framework.

evidence_quote: Continuity planning involves identifying your unit’s Critical functions, Key resources, Key decision makers, Information technology tools and building a plan for how you would protect those functions in the event of a disruption.
catalog_notes: Office of Emergency Management runs UD's COOP program and maintains the University Emergency Management/COOP Policy. Program page cites recent disruptions (Winter Storm Fern 2026, COVID-19) and links to the general counsel Emergency Management Policy. Department COOP plans drafted via OEM-supported form.

framework_alignment: FAMU CEMP; Florida Statutes Chapter 252 Emergency Management; BOG Regulation 1.001
evidence_quote: To ensure the safety of our University community and continuity of operations and facilities, this Policy provides guidance to departments on identifying essential employees that are designated to work during a University emergency and/or closure.
catalog_notes: Florida A&M (an HBCU). Signed University Policy UP-03-03 Essential Personnel Designation (read in full incl. Appendix 1 essential-personnel roster). Defines Critical Personnel and First Responders, FAMU EOC activation, Incident Command structure, and references the FAMU CEMP and Chapter 252. The detailed CEMP/COOP itself is not posted publicly.

framework_alignment: FAU University Policy 3.4.1, Emergency Management; CEMP alignment
evidence_quote: At a minimum, UCOOP submissions must address the following: Succession planning; Notification/Communication procedures; Identification of essential functions; Establishment of means to support essential functions (i.e., space, equipment, databases); and Reconstitution procedures.
catalog_notes: FAU Department of Emergency Management requires every unit to file a Unit Continuity of Operations Plan (UCOOP) using the current published template (separate Colleges and General templates, DOCX). Unit Planning page (read directly) lists the required UCOOP elements (succession, communications, essential functions, alternate means, reconstitution); completed unit plans are access-restricted.

framework_alignment: Florida Statutes Section 252.365
evidence_quote: A COOP plan is a collection of resources, actions, procedures, and information that is developed, tested, and held in readiness for use in the event of a major disruption of operations. COOP planning helps prepare University units to maintain mission critical operations after any emergency or disaster. COOP planning is required by Florida Statutes Section 252.365.
catalog_notes: FGCU Emergency Management COOP program page (read directly): living document tested at least annually; plans contain sensitive information and are protected from release under Florida Sunshine Laws. Statutorily mandated under FL 252.365.

framework_alignment: Florida Statutes Chapter 252; Comprehensive Emergency Management Plan (CEMP)
evidence_quote: Florida International University faces the risks and consequences of natural, technological and operational emergencies that may result in loss of life, property damage or interruption of essential functions of the University community.
catalog_notes: FIU Policy 180.105 'Emergency Management and Continuity of Operations Policy' (published 2020-06-11). Establishes the FIU Comprehensive Emergency Management Plan based on Florida Statutes Ch. 252; assures continuity and protection of essential functions. FIU also operates the 'FIU Ready' unit-level COOP planning tool (Department of Emergency Management), which maintains COOPs for all FIU departments.

framework_alignment: FCD-style continuity elements; all-hazards; FSU Policy 4-OP-E-10 / 4-OP-H-5 / 4-OP-H-10
evidence_quote: This Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) based on essential functions, takes an all-hazards approach in preparing for natural, man-made, and technological incidents.
catalog_notes: University-published COOP template for units; mandated by FSU Policy 4-OP-E-10. Includes a continuity-of-research section. Administered by the Emergency Management Division.

framework_alignment: Florida Statute 252.365; Florida BOG Regulation 3.001; FEMA IS-1300
evidence_quote: To maintain a viable COOP capability, each department's or unit's plan must: be maintained at a high level of readiness; be capable of implementation, both with and without warning; be operational no later than 12 hours after activation; maintain sustained operations for up to 30 days; include a business impact analysis; take maximum advantage of existing agency field infrastructures.
catalog_notes: UCF Policy 3-117.2 (read in full) defines the COOP program: BIA required, 12-hour/30-day RTO, alternate facilities, interoperable communications, vital records/databases. Department COOP coordinators must complete FEMA IS-1300. Detailed unit plans are protected under the Florida Sunshine Laws and not posted; this policy is the publicly posted governing document.

framework_alignment: UF CEMP all-hazards framework; national best practices and industry standards

Documented as the institution's own published template for its units.

framework_alignment: UM Comprehensive Emergency Management Plan; UReady continuity planning system

framework_alignment: COOP essential-functions model; 30-day sustained operations

framework_alignment: TCSG State Board Policy 3.4.1

framework_alignment: TCSG Policy II.D Emergency Preparedness, Health, Safety and Security

framework_alignment: Institute business continuity / disaster recovery standard set by OEM
evidence_quote: Georgia Tech Units shall develop, regularly maintain, and exercise business continuity and disaster recovery plans. ... Each Unit shall conduct and document at least one continuity exercise annually with key Unit stakeholders.
catalog_notes: Georgia Tech Business Continuity Policy (read in full): OEM-set standard system, identification/ranking of critical functions, mitigation strategies for loss of infrastructure/personnel/financial resources, annual updates and annual exercises, Continuity Plan Owners/Managers, and IT disaster recovery. The 'Business Continuity' definition explicitly references identifying the impact of potential losses.

framework_alignment: Mission Essential Functions (MEFs); 12 hour - 30 day continuity framework
evidence_quote: COOP plans identify the organization's Mission Essential Functions (MEFs) and the critical personnel, resources, systems, and processes necessary to maintain them, and establish policy and guidance to ensure that MEFs are prioritized over all other functions during operational disruptions, are resumed within 12 hours and maintained for up to 30 days.
catalog_notes: Georgia Southern Office of Emergency Management Emergency Plans page resolves on the official georgiasouthern.edu host. Quote captured from the search-result snippet; direct page render was blocked at access time (the related finops.georgiasouthern.edu continuity-plans page sits behind the campus CAS login). COOP described around MEFs with a 12-hour/30-day framework. Verify on next direct render.

framework_alignment: NIMS / ICS; all-hazards emergency operations
evidence_quote: Georgia State University is in the process of developing a Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) to allow for a quick recovery of the campus community from an emergency or disaster situation.
catalog_notes: Georgia State University 2022-2023 Emergency Operations Plan PDF resolves on the official risk.gsu.edu host. Quote captured from the search-result snippet describing the COOP within GSU's emergency-management program; direct PDF render not completed at access time due to fetch rate limiting. Office of Emergency Management (safety.gsu.edu/oem) maintains university-wide plans.

framework_alignment: FEMA preparedness/response/recovery/mitigation phases; Owl Ready continuity platform
evidence_quote: The major document that is used in recovery is the Continuity of Operations Plan, or COOP. The purpose of the COOP is to ensure continued operations of critical functions in the event of an emergency and to ensure a rapid response to any emergency situation requiring continuity plan implementation. The COOP contains administrative and academic department plans that allow them, and their respective sub-departments, to define their critical assets, personnel, and functions.
catalog_notes: KSU Office of Emergency Management Recovery page (read directly) describes the COOP as the major recovery document built around departmental plans into a university-wide approach; uses the cloud-based 'Owl Ready' (Kuali Ready) continuity planning application. Covers both administrative and academic department continuity.

framework_alignment: All-hazards departmental business continuity model
evidence_quote: Business Continuity Planning is the practice of planning how your department will provide services or conduct departmental business during or after an emergency or disaster that may have both short-term and long-term consequences. The Departmental Level Business Continuity Planning Tool was created to assist University of Georgia (UGA) departments in the development of business continuity plans specific to their unique departmental and building needs.
catalog_notes: UGA Office of Emergency Preparedness operates a web-based BCP Tool Online Plan Generator (eight sections) producing department-specific BCPs; completed plans are restricted and not posted publicly. The public BCP Checklist PDF (https://prepare.uga.edu/wp-content/uploads/BCP-Handout-April-2014.pdf) was also read and covers mission-critical function prioritization, data/technology recovery, vital records, and annual test/maintenance.

framework_alignment: TCSG Procedure 7.2.1p2; State of Georgia Living Disaster Recovery Planning System (LDRPS)
evidence_quote: The Business Continuity Plan (BCP) ensures the maintenance of critical operations when confronted with adverse events such as natural disasters, technology failures, human errors, or terrorism. The BCP must minimize loss to the organization, continue to serve customers, and maintain administrative operations.
catalog_notes: Published institutional procedure (7.2.1p2) establishing the BCP requirement, with the blank TCSG Business Continuity Plan Template attached. Procedure prescribes performing a Business Impact Analysis, completing a Hazard Vulnerability Assessment, and training/drilling/exercising participants; BCP submitted to the TCSG System Office annually by May 1.

framework_alignment: Departmental Emergency Continuity of Operations Plan (DECOP) program
evidence_quote: Departments or units should each have an emergency plan that details the functions in their area that must continue if the university suspends activities and closes for an extended period.
catalog_notes: Environmental Health and Safety Department/Unit Emergency Plan page, part of ISU's Departmental Emergency Continuity of Operations Plan (DECOP) program. Key plan elements listed: leadership succession; essential personnel and operational functions; communication and information management; key internal/external dependencies; recovery plan.

framework_alignment: Kuali Ready (higher-ed continuity planning tool)
evidence_quote: For purposes of planning at the University of Iowa, business continuity is defined as the continuity of critical functions following an incident that overwhelms normal everyday operations.
catalog_notes: Risk Management page describing the institution's business continuity program; units build departmental continuity of operations plans through the university's Kuali Ready subscription. Distinguishes business continuity from disaster recovery and emergency preparedness.

framework_alignment: FEMA continuity concepts (criticality tiers)
evidence_quote: A Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) documents how an organization will perform essential operations during an emergency situation or long-term disruption, which might last 2 days to several weeks.
catalog_notes: Office of Emergency Management guidance for units to build an abbreviated COOP. Covers succession planning (at least three deep), essential personnel/functions with criticality levels (Critical 1-3, Deferrable), and mitigation strategies including online-learning continuity. Companion to Boise State's 2021 Hazard Mitigation Plan.

evidence_quote: The purpose of the Policy is to provide reasonable, but not absolute, assurance that DePaul's business will continue as soon as possible after any incident that disrupts some or all of the university's essential business operations.
catalog_notes: Business Continuity Management Policy defining a six-step unit process (BIA, risk assessment, risk reduction, BCP development with succession plans and alternate facilities, training, biennial exercise). Establishes RTOs and RPOs.

evidence_quote: A Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) addresses these topics and generally covers a short to mid-term range (roughly 1-30 days). Long-term recovery efforts, such as rebuilding facilities, are addressed in separate recovery plans.
catalog_notes: University Continuity Program coordinated by Emergency Management, guided by a cross-divisional Continuity Steering Committee; spans academic, research, and administrative continuity plus a separately charged IT Disaster Recovery Plan led by an IT DR Advisory Board.

plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
framework_alignment: Activated under the college Emergency Response Plan (ERP)
evidence_quote: The Continuity of Academic Operations Plan (CAOP) is activated only after college officials have declared an emergency and activated the college’s Emergency Response Plan (ERP). The CAOP will not be activated without an official ERP activation.
catalog_notes: Faculty Quick Reference for the Continuity of Academic Operations Plan. Defines phased instructor actions keyed to disruption length (1-5 days, 6-10 days, 10+ days) and use of the LMS, email, and alternate course management to maintain instruction; 10+ day disruptions trigger an administrative viability determination for the semester.

evidence_quote: Business continuity refers to Northwestern's ability to maintain operations during and after an emergency or crisis event. If business continuity is effectively in place, Northwestern's key academic, research, and administrative functions will be minimally impacted by a disruptive incident.
catalog_notes: Business continuity coordinated, facilitated, and overseen by the Risk Management Office; all administrative, academic, and research areas are responsible for developing a business continuity plan. Dedicated Business Continuity Planning Office.

evidence_quote: UIC's approach to organizational resilience is grounded in a two-part planning process supported by the Riskonnect BC&R platform. This ensures our critical functions are protected and our campus is prepared to respond quickly and effectively to unexpected disruptions.
catalog_notes: Office of Preparedness and Response Business Continuity & Resilience program using the Riskonnect BC&R platform; two-part process of Business Impact Analysis (BIA) and Business Continuity Planning (BCP) across academic, research, healthcare, and administrative units.

framework_alignment: All-hazards
evidence_quote: It is impossible to plan for everything, so the university has taken an "all-hazards" approach to continuity planning — meaning we plan for restoring services no matter what the cause. The university and all colleges, departments, and units on campus are required to develop and maintain a business continuity plan.
catalog_notes: Division of Public Safety / Emergency Management continuity planning program. Units build plans in the web-based Illinois Ready (Kuali Ready) tool; living document with annual testing (tabletop/exercise/drill).

evidence_quote: A Business Continuity Plan (BCP) takes a snapshot of everything your department does on an annual basis, with a list of the resources it needs to function. The point of a BCP is to address abnormal situations and then get you back on track to operating as normal.
catalog_notes: University-wide BCP program coordinated by Emergency Management & Continuity; every department on every campus is required to have a BCP, built in the IU Ready (Kuali Ready) tool.

framework_alignment: NIMS / Incident Command System
evidence_quote: The plan's critical goals are the preservation of life, the protection of property, and continuity of academic and business operations.
catalog_notes: Continuity of academic and business operations is a named critical goal of Purdue's Integrated Emergency Management Plan (IEMP), an all-hazards NIMS/ICS-based plan reviewed annually; the IEMP also promotes a means to recover mission-critical business and academic operations. Quote taken from official purdue.edu IEMP PDF via search snippet; the large PDF returned a fetch rate-limit/size error at time of access.

evidence_quote: Business Continuity Plans contain a collection of resources, procedures, actions and information that have been developed, tested, and are held ready to use in the event of a disaster or major disruption of operations.
catalog_notes: Published Business Continuity Program (policy SAFE028) with Appendix A list of departments required to plan and Appendix B BCP template focused on essential functions, RTO ('Maximum Time to go without Essential Function'), communications, and supplier MOUs.

system_affiliation: U.S. Bureau of Indian Education (federal TCU)
evidence_quote: University departments are responsible for developing emergency response and continuity of operations plans for their areas and staff. Campus emergency management provides resources and guidance for the development of these plans.
catalog_notes: Haskell Indian Nations University (federally operated BIE institution) 2025 Annual Security & Fire Safety Report. Maintains an Emergency Response Plan and assigns university departments responsibility for developing continuity of operations plans, with campus emergency management providing guidance and annual exercises. Continuity-of-operations requirement is documented in the ASR rather than as a separately posted standalone COOP.

framework_alignment: State of Kansas COOP program; FEMA all-hazards
evidence_quote: Continuity of Operations Plans (COOPs) allow the university to continue to provide essential services in the event of a disruption. Continuity plans identify critical resources and staff, identify alternate facilities, lines of succession, and delegation procedures for university staff and services.
catalog_notes: KU Police Department Emergency Management pre-incident planning page. KU participates in a state-wide COOP project customized for Kansas; COOPs created for the university as a whole and for individual departments. Page also references the 2024 Integrated Preparedness Plan.

framework_alignment: BOLDplanning; tied to Emergency Operations Plan (Level 2/3 emergencies)
evidence_quote: The Business Continuity Plan was developed in order to ensure the University has the ability to maintain critical services and functions in the event of a disaster or other disruption of service.
catalog_notes: WSU Business Continuity Plan page (Finance). Could not render page bytes during this session due to a fetch rate limit; URL resolves on the official wichita.edu host and the quoted text is reproduced in the search result snippet. Senior executives/managers must create business continuity and recovery plans protecting essential records and programs; plan includes risk assessments, recovery plans identifying resources/vital records, and regularly scheduled exercising. A standing Business Continuity Committee supports the President's Executive Team.

framework_alignment: All-hazards; required departmental BCPs; UK Police Crisis Management and Preparedness
evidence_quote: As you pursue academic and research excellence on campus, all UK departments and units are required to complete a BCP to ensure your department is prepared to respond to various types of operational interruptions. This BCP will help you plan not only for major disasters (e.g. total loss of a building) but also lesser interruptions to service (e.g., the computers are down).
catalog_notes: University of Kentucky requires all departments/units to complete a Business Continuity Plan (BCP). Administered by UK Police Department's Crisis Management and Preparedness division with a Business Continuity Coordinator. This is UK's operational continuity program (distinct from the UK academic Continuity of Instruction guidelines record).

plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
framework_alignment: Academic continuity / continuity of instruction; remote-delivery contingency (Canvas, Zoom, YuJa)
evidence_quote: University of Kentucky Continuity of Instruction Guidelines for Teaching During Disruptions or Closure This document is presented as a starting place for colleges and other academic units to determine plans for continuing the instruction of courses if there were temporary disruptions to the academic schedule or a campus closure.
catalog_notes: UK 'Continuity of Instruction' guidelines (Teaching, Learning, and Academic Innovation, Provost area). College-wide faculty survey, deans/chairs checklist, remote-delivery scenarios (short-term closure, semester closure), synchronous/asynchronous instruction. Distinct academic-continuity document; UK also requires a separate Business Continuity Plan (BCP) program via UK Police Crisis Management & Preparedness.

framework_alignment: BIA + RTO/RPO; gap analysis; risk assessment; IT disaster recovery; annual testing
evidence_quote: Effective business continuity and disaster recovery plans are required in all areas of the University. Each school, unit and division must develop plans that will allow it to perform its core-required operations in an alternative fashion as well as an appropriate disaster recovery policy for their working environment. ... An effective Business Continuity Plan (BCP) contains the steps outlined below ... Perform Gap Analysis. Conduct Risk Assessment. Perform Business Impact Analysis. Determine Continuity/Recovery Strategy.
catalog_notes: UofL administrative policy ISO-002 'Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery' (Information Security Compliance Office). Defines BCP steps including gap analysis, risk assessment, BIA with RTO and RPO, off-site storage, annual training/testing and annual plan review. Required for all schools/units/divisions.

plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
framework_alignment: LSU Policy Statements 22, 45, 117; instructional continuity guidance
evidence_quote: The purpose of this plan is to provide guidance to LSU faculty and academic administration on the continuation of the university's academic mission during campus closures when classes are in session. ... this plan provides flexibility for LSU to pivot to alternative forms of instruction rather than cancel classes and schedule make-up classes on weekends or during breaks.
catalog_notes: LSU Office of Academic Affairs Academic Continuity Plan (read in full): covers communications/closure decision-making (President + EVPs + Campus Safety AVP), alternative instruction modes (Zoom/Moodle), accessibility/accommodations, student absences, and research continuity through the LSU EOC. Distinct from the access-restricted LSU OEP operational plans.

framework_alignment: Recovery of critical activities/functions after business interruption
evidence_quote: The purpose of Tulane University Continuity of Operations (COOP) is to coordinate Tulane's recovery operations after a business interruption and return university operations to normal as soon as possible. The objective of COOP is to coordinate recovery of critical activities in support of the university in the event of an incident that interrupts critical functions.
catalog_notes: Tulane (private R1, New Orleans) Emergency Preparedness & Response operates a COOP program; departments/schools build COOP plans with planning-assistance requests. Page read directly. Companion IT Hurricane and Disaster Recovery Plan referenced via Tulane IT.

framework_alignment: NIMS/ICS; FEMA continuity
evidence_quote: Critical Functions and Recovery Time Objectives (RTO), or the time by which an organization must be able to resume critical operations, is a major factor in planning recovery.
catalog_notes: Boston College's own published Departmental Business Continuity Plan template/sample (Jesuit, Catholic). Structured around critical functions and RTO, software applications, vital records, orders of succession, emergency/alternate operating facilities, notification/communication, and an 81-item recovery checklist. Built on NIMS/ICS section structure (Operations/Logistics/Planning/Finance). Marked 'CONFIDENTIAL--Not for External Distribution' but publicly posted as the unit template.

framework_alignment: Boston College Business Continuity Policy (President-approved 2015)
evidence_quote: This Policy is promulgated to establish general University-wide standards for business continuity planning so as to minimize disruption to critical University operations. It applies to all University Administrative/Academic Units at whatever location they operate.
catalog_notes: University-wide Business Continuity Policy (distinct from the unit template). Mandates each unit's BCP include critical functions and resources with a prioritized recovery checklist, an alternative staffing plan and line of succession, an alternative location, and a communication plan; plans updated at least annually; OEM reviews. References University-wide IT disaster recovery responsibilities.

framework_alignment: FEMA COOP concepts (essential functions, alternate facility, orders of succession)
evidence_quote: A continuity of operations plan establishes policy and guidance ensuring that critical functions continue and that personnel and resources are relocated to an alternate facility in case of emergencies.
catalog_notes: BU Emergency Management COOP program page with a downloadable BU-COOP-Plan-Template (v7) for departments. Twelve-step planning process covering mission, prioritized critical functions, recovery locations, continuity of authority, essential staff, critical records, computer inventory, IT needs, and communication tools. Departmental tool designed for higher education.

framework_alignment: Organizational resiliency; Veoci platform
evidence_quote: Business continuity is a process that builds organizational resiliency so that critical operations and services are able to resume and continue under adverse or abnormal conditions.
catalog_notes: Emerson College (specialized communication/arts institution) Emergency Management business continuity program. Departments build and maintain business continuity plans in an online planning application (Veoci) to sustain critical operations and minimize disruption impact.

framework_alignment: Emergency management cycle (response-to-recovery); Critical Position Policy
evidence_quote: Focuses on the planning efforts needed to ensure Harvard's critical functions remain operational during and following disruptive incidents. Business Continuity is part of the emergency management cycle that connects the emergency response phase to the recovery phase.
catalog_notes: Harvard Environmental Health & Safety / Emergency Management Business Continuity program. Provides central tools, templates, an online application, and support to schools and departments developing their own BC programs and plans. References a Critical Position Policy. Program led by a Business Continuity Manager.

framework_alignment: Essential-functions continuity (mission continuity)
evidence_quote: To ensure that the Institute can continue its mission after a disruption, MIT Emergency Management collaborates with departments, labs, and centers (DLCs) to develop continuity plans. The goal of a continuity plan is to ensure that essential functions will continue after a disruption.
catalog_notes: MIT Emergency Management continuity (mission continuity) program. Defines continuity and essential functions; provides a Mission Continuity Worksheet for departments/labs/centers (DLCs) and an Individual Continuity Worksheet. Specialized research-intensive institution.

framework_alignment: NIMS/ICS; all-hazards; Enterprise Risk Management
evidence_quote: Smith's Department of Emergency Management strives to help the college plan and prepare for major disasters or emergencies that would impact the college's community, business operations, or academic operations.
catalog_notes: Smith College (women's liberal arts) Enterprise Risk Management / Emergency Management page. Key services include developing, maintaining, and testing a College Emergency Operations Plan and an Incident Management Team addressing the college's community, business continuity, and academic programs, using Incident Command System principles and an all-hazards approach. Insurance/risk via the Five College Office of Risk Management.

framework_alignment: All-hazards continuity; Kuali Ready platform
evidence_quote: This document provides planning and guidance for implementing this Continuity Plan to ensure the organization is capable of conducting its essential functions under all threats and conditions.
catalog_notes: Published excerpts of the University Continuity of Operations Plan (full plan held by the Office of Emergency Management). All-hazards, essential-functions based, with continuity priorities (critical 1/2/3 functions), an Executive Policy Group, Emergency Manager/EOC roles, and academic continuity (high-priority courses). Units build plans in Kuali (Tufts Continuity Planner).

framework_alignment: FEMA and MEMA standards; all-hazards; Board of Trustees Policy T99-060
evidence_quote: Each campus will maintain continuity of operations plans and capabilities to ensure the campus can continue operations during times of emergency or compromised operations. Business continuity plans should identify functions and operations that must continue during times of emergency or compromised operations, as well as the resources needed to support these operations.
catalog_notes: University of Massachusetts President's Office Administrative Standards for the Emergency Management and Business Continuity Policy (Doc. T99-060), effective 2024. Systemwide, all-hazards approach guided by FEMA and MEMA standards; requires each campus to maintain a CEMP and continuity-of-operations plans/capabilities; coordinated via systemwide Enterprise Risk Management.

framework_alignment: Continuity planning; risk identification and safeguards
evidence_quote: When an emergency takes place and the university is closed or has limited access, departments should refer to their individual continuity of operations plans. Continuity planning is defined as an effort to define potential risks, determine how those risks will affect the operations of our campus, and implement safeguards designed to diminish those risks.
catalog_notes: UMass Boston Office of Emergency Management and Business Continuity continuity-planning program. Provides a Business Continuity Planning Policy, a Business Continuity Planning Form, and a 'How to Identify Critical Functions' resource. Provost's Office oversees instructional, research, and academic continuity. Departments maintain individual COOPs.

framework_alignment: UMass Board of Trustees Business Continuity and Planning Policy (Doc. T99-060)
evidence_quote: The President of the University shall develop and adopt guidelines to require each campus to develop, implement and test business continuity plans for its critical business processes and data systems.
catalog_notes: UMass Dartmouth's adoption of the University of Massachusetts Board of Trustees Business Continuity and Planning Policy (Doc. T99-060). Requires each campus to develop, implement, and test BCPs for critical business processes and data systems; rank/prioritize critical systems; assess risk of disruption; and formulate prompt-resumption plans. IT/data-systems focus.

framework_alignment: UMass President's Office Emergency Management and Business Continuity standards
evidence_quote: Continuity planning is an effort to ensure that essential functions of the University can continue across a wide range of potential emergencies. Continuity Planning at UMass is designed to address three types of disruptions: Denial/loss of access to a facility (such as damage to the building); Service interruption due to a reduced workforce (such as a communicable disease); and Service interruption due to equipment or systems failure (such as IT systems failure).
catalog_notes: UMass Lowell Controller's Office business continuity planning page. Identifies twelve critical business continuity development areas designated by the UMass President's Office (Emergency Management & BC, Controller, Budget/Admin & Finance, Communications, EH&S, Executive Office, HR, IT, Police, Procurement, Facilities, Residential Life). Addresses facility-denial, reduced-workforce, and equipment/IT-failure disruptions.

framework_alignment: Business Continuity Management Process (pre-incident planning, incident response, plan development)
evidence_quote: Business Continuity Plan is the process whereby organizations ensure the maintenance of critical operations when confronted with adverse events such as natural disasters, technology failures, human errors, or terrorism. The objectives of a business continuity plan are to minimize loss of the organization, continue to serve customers, and maintain administrative operations.
catalog_notes: University-wide BCP with detailed Business Impact Analysis tables by division (allowable downtime and recovery priority), hazard matrix/risk assessment, and unit-plan testing/maintenance requirements (exercised at least every two years). DIT maintains a separate Disaster Recovery Plan.

plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
evidence_quote: such unexpected events can disrupt face-to-face instruction. Coppin is outfitted with diverse instructional tools that can minimize instructional impact in the event of class cancellations.
catalog_notes: Coppin State 'Instructional Continuity Resources for Faculty' page (Information Technology Division). Includes 'Strategies for Preserving Instructional Continuity' and 'Preparedness Recommendations' (e.g., share emergency plans with students) using Blackboard Learn/Collaborate for remote instruction during disruptions.

framework_alignment: References FEMA IS-1300 Introduction to Continuity
evidence_quote: Department plans should be prepared with the goal of enabling the University's essential functions to continue regardless of whether or not systems are operational, facilities and infrastructure services are available, or other organizations are viable.
catalog_notes: Institution-published departmental Business Continuity Plan template (Jesuit university). Captures emergency contacts, office equipment, critical/essential operations in order of importance, readiness checklist, essential resources/supplies/services, and chair/director sign-off. Stored in SharePoint; all campus departments required to complete a BCP.

framework_alignment: FEMA four-phase continuity model (Readiness/Preparedness; Activation/Relocation; Continuity Operations; Reconstitution)
evidence_quote: This Continuity of Operations (COOP) Plan provides the overarching program management framework for the University of Maryland, Baltimore (UMB) Continuity Program. It identifies how the Office of Emergency Management (OEM) supports offices and schools (herein known as “departments”) plan and prepare to continue performing the most essential work despite interruptions and impacts to, “people, places, and things”.
catalog_notes: Program-management framework built around People/Places/Things and four continuity phases. Uses Veoci for plan maintenance; CORE departments revise every two years. Health-sciences-focused campus (medicine, nursing, law, dentistry, pharmacy, social work). Includes Alternate Space Requirements Tool and Essential Program Functions list.

framework_alignment: USM Policy VI-13.00; State of Maryland COOP Guide; essential-functions hierarchy (SEF/IEF/UEF/PMEF/MEF/ESA/MEA)
evidence_quote: This COOP instructs each College, Division, and subordinate unit on the process for identifying essential functions. It describes the levels of essential functions, identifies the University’s Essential Functions, and states how they align with Institutional Essential Functions from USM and State Essential Functions from the State of Maryland.
catalog_notes: Exceptionally detailed 30-page COOP as Annex 3 of the Base EOP. Four-phase continuity process (identify MEFs, BPA, BIA, prioritize/document); defines MTD, RTO, RPO; aligns with USM VI-13.00 and Maryland State COOP Guide. Includes Continuity Working Group, DEAPs, and MEF/BPA data sheet templates. Marked For Official Use Only but publicly posted.

framework_alignment: University System of Maryland COOP requirement
evidence_quote: Continuity of Operations (COOP), also referred to as business continuity, is the ability to continue to perform critical activities when normal operating conditions are disrupted (e.g., key staff are ill, the office loses power). The University System of Maryland (USM) requires COOP plans to ensure its institutions can maintain their critical functions when faced with unexpected challenges.
catalog_notes: Office of Emergency Management and Business Continuity (OEMBC) administers the COOP program; created a department COOP template centered on critical activities. Campus EOP (2022) is the foundation. USM mandates COOP plans.

framework_alignment: All-hazards
evidence_quote: COOP is an effort within individual departments and agencies to ensure continuity of their essential functions across a wide range of emergencies and events.
catalog_notes: EMU Emergency Management Office COOP program; one of a network of plans (Comprehensive Emergency Management Plan, COOP, Building Emergency Plans, Multi-Hazard Mitigation Plan). Units complete COOPs from templates with EMO help. Quote taken from official emich.edu page via search snippet; page returned a fetch rate-limit error at time of access.

institution_name: University of Michigan (Human Research Protection Program)
evidence_quote: This Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) is meant to guide the development of contingency plans to support operations in the Human Research Protection Program (HRPP) during an emergency, disaster, or significant disruption to university systems affecting institutional or research operations.
catalog_notes: Department-level COOP for U-M's HRPP/IRB operations. Notes all U-M units are expected to maintain plans covering essential functions, staffing, supplies, communications, and contingency planning. Tied to Regents SPG 201.27 emergency reduction in operations.

evidence_quote: The Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) is a tool used to help each department or business unit continue to function as a viable part of the entire organization in the event of an emergency.
catalog_notes: Emergency-management COOP program page. All units required to complete and submit a COOP, identifying critical processes, analyzing business impacts, mitigating risks, and exercising the plan.

evidence_quote: A continuity planning template has been designed by the University for departments to use as a guide or initial starting point when beginning the process of developing their own continuity of operations plans.
catalog_notes: EHS-run annual COOP review program with a university-designed continuity planning template (linked Google Doc) for departments. Guidance covers critical operations, remote work, IT dependencies, relocation needs, and alternate vendors.

institution_name: Minnesota State Colleges and Universities (System Office)
framework_alignment: Board Policy 5.24 (Safety and Security Compliance); System Procedure 5.24.3

Requires each institution and the system office to use a COOP template (and EOP template) from the Public Safety & Compliance SharePoint.

framework_alignment: Minnesota State System Procedure 5.24.3 (Emergency Management)
evidence_quote: The Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) is designed to describe how departments will operate during an emergency and recover afterwards.
catalog_notes: University Security / Emergency Preparedness COOP page. Each department/division completes a COOP using a provided DOCX template and submits to the Campus Emergency Manager.

framework_alignment: NIMS; FEMA continuity terminology
evidence_quote: An effort within individual departments to ensure the continued performance of minimum essential functions during a wide range of potential emergencies. This is accomplished through development of the plans, comprehensive procedures, and provision for alternate facilities, personnel, resources, interoperable communications, and vital records/databases.
catalog_notes: Administrative Policy establishing the COOP program for Critical Operating Units (COUs), coordinated by the Department of Emergency Management; requires DR plans for units identified by Information Security policy. Includes a downloadable COOP template (DOCX) for all units.

framework_alignment: NIMS / ICS; all-hazards (L363 Multi-Hazards EM for Higher Ed)
evidence_quote: Continual MSU operations are critical when impacted by a large-scale incident. There are key principles that must be planned and practiced avoiding any significant disruptions with MSU operations.
catalog_notes: Missouri State University EOP (2023 Basic Plan) contains a dedicated Section VII 'CONTINUITY OF OPERATIONS' (BP 12) with subsections A 'Cancellation of Classes and Campus Closure' (academic continuity) and B 'Preservation of Records' (vital records). Distinct institution from the University of Missouri System already cataloged. Confirmed by reading the rendered PDF.

framework_alignment: NIST-style BIA / Information System Contingency Plan terminology
evidence_quote: Business continuity planning includes the identification of vulnerabilities, priorities, dependencies, and measures required to facilitate continuity and recovery before, during, and after a crisis.
catalog_notes: System-level IT Business Continuity Management program run by Information Security and Access Management (ISAM). Specifies Business Impact Analysis (BIA) and Information System Contingency Plan (ISCP) as minimum procedures, plus a TT&E plan; systems classified by criticality.

framework_alignment: Level-based emergency response (Levels 1-3)
evidence_quote: Members are responsible for ensuring that their functional area has a Critical Operations Plan and necessary resources to execute the plan.
catalog_notes: Alcorn State Emergency Management Plan (Revised Fall 2021) organized by Levels 1-3 with EMT/EOT structure. Each functional area must maintain a 'Critical Operations Plan'; the plan includes a Campus Recovery section and satellite/communication continuity provisions. Primarily an emergency management plan with embedded continuity-of-operations elements rather than a standalone COOP.

framework_alignment: Incident Command System (ICS)
evidence_quote: The Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) is intended to establish policies, procedures and organizational structure for response to emergencies that are of sufficient magnitude to cause a significant disruption of the functioning of all or portions of Itawamba Community College.
catalog_notes: 67-page institution-wide COOP.
